Goblin Calculator

know your real profit before you list it
1 FLOWER = Coins
Seeds and tools are bought with Coins, but items sell for FLOWER. This rate converts Coin cost into FLOWER so profit is calculated on the same currency.
Spent FLOWER for Gems
Gems get cheaper the more you buy at once (bonding curve pricing), so there's no single fixed rate. Open the Buy Gems → FLOWER purchase screen, pick whatever bundle you actually buy, and enter the exact FLOWER cost and Gems amount shown there. The ratio below recalculates live — re-enter these anytime you buy a different (discounted) bundle to keep it accurate.
Tools always cost 10 Gems to restock — that doesn't change no matter which button you pick. Seeds cost 15 Gems restocked alone, or effectively 10 Gems when bundled into a 20-Gem "All" restock (20 ÷ 2). Pick "All" only if you actually need both a tool and a seed restock at the same time — otherwise you're paying for a restock you didn't need. This choice feeds directly into every 24h profit calc.
%
Charged only when you sell, based on your current in-game reputation tier. Check your farm's marketplace screen for the exact number.
What each item currently sells for on the marketplace, in FLOWER. Every seed, tool, or animal produce you add above shows up here automatically — set or update its price any time and every Harvested item recalculates instantly. Delete the source above to remove it from here too.
🔄 Live prices not synced yet
The base game cost of every tool, crop, fruit, resource and animal — what it produces and how much, loaded automatically. Nothing here is hand-editable: it's the game's own numbers. When a boost changes a cost or a yield, the new number is shown with the original struck through and the boost named next to it.
🔨 Tools
Crops
Fruits
Greenhouse
Resources
Animals
Honey & Flowers
Crops (auto)
All base crops, loaded automatically — cost, yield & profit update live with your boosts and plot count. Edit sell price in Marketplace Prices below.
Resources & Tools (auto)
Wood → Stone → Iron → Gold → Crimstone → Oil. Tap a resource to see its tool's full recipe. Choose Collect or Buy per material.
Fruits (auto)
Tomato, Lemon, Blueberry, Orange, Apple & Banana — cost, yield & profit update live with your boosts. Grapes are excluded (greenhouse crop). Edit sell price in Marketplace Prices below.
Composter (auto)
Compost Bin, Turbo Composter & Premium Composter — not tradable, but every cycle turns crops/fruit (+ sometimes Eggs) into a fertiliser you can apply to a growing crop or fruit tree. Ingredient requirements change every season, so cost per fertiliser updates automatically with the season toggle above. Worms are a free by-product (fishing bait) — not counted as cost. Turn fertilisers on/off per-crop-type in ⚡ Boost Settings → 🪱 Composter to see their yield/time effect and coin cost show up on the Crops & Fruits cards.
Honey & Flowers (auto)
Every hive is paired to a flower bed. As long as that bed keeps a flower growing, the hive fills with Honey over 24h (boostable). Flowers themselves aren't tradable — only the Honey a full hive produces is. Set your Hives count below (1 per flower bed), pick which flower you keep replanting, and the numbers update live. Seed & ingredient costs are editable estimates — correct them to match your farmer's market prices.
🔍 Flower Finder
Pick a Seed and what you're pairing it with — see exactly which flower grows, and what it costs (using its saved pairing below).
Flowers
Grouped by seed. Each variety's true cost = that seed's coin cost + whatever you paired it with — if the pairing is itself a flower, its own cost is priced in too, so rarer chained flowers cost more than ones grown straight from a crop.
Animals (auto)
Chicken, Sheep & Cow — set level and head count in ⚡ Boosts → Plots & Nodes. Cost, feed and profit update live with your boosts, feed ingredient choice (Collect/Buy) and consumables/medicine.
Salt (auto)
The Salt Awakening chapter: rake Salt from Salt Nodes, upgrade the Sculpture for bonuses, then run the Aging Rack → Fermentation Rack → Spice Rack chain to turn Salt into tradable Baits and upgraded Fertilisers. Salt and Baits are tradable — edit sell price in 🛒 Marketplace Prices. Fertilisers made here are an upgrade of your existing Composter fertilisers — turning one on in 🪱 Composter automatically turns its classic counterpart off (and vice versa), since only one fertiliser can sit on a plot at a time.
🥒 Vegs (pickling cost, wired to Salt)
🧪 Aging Rack & Fermentation Rack — Baits (tradable)
🌿 Fermentation Rack — Fertilisers (applies in 🪱 Composter, not tradable)
🥫 Spice Rack
Greenhouse (auto)
Grape, Rice & Olive — grown in the Greenhouse building. Cost = Seed (coins) + Oil (always collected — Oil can't be bought). Set your plot count per crop in ⚡ Boosts → Plots & Nodes. All three are tradable — edit sell price in Marketplace Prices below.
Crop Machine (auto)
Queue seed packs, add Oil, and the machine grows them in bulk over a fixed runtime — no plots needed. Sunflower, Potato & Pumpkin are unlocked from the start; the rest need the matching Crop Machine module skill (tick the box below once you actually own it in-game). Oil cost pulls straight from your ⛏️ Resources → 🛢️ Oil boosts; crop yield pulls from your 🌾 Crops boosts.

👺 A Word From Da Goblin

Oi oi, welcome traveler! Before ya start clickin' buttons and countin' coins, lil' Goblin's gotta say his piece.

GOBLIN CALCULATOR is NOT official, not affiliated, not secretly run by them big Sunflower Land folks — nope, just a scrappy lil goblin app made by, well... a goblin. All the credit for Sunflower Land itself (the real game, the real farm, the real fun) goes to them. Go give the real bosses a visit: sunflower-land.com

Every pretty picture, icon, and lil sprite floatin' around this app? Belongs to Sunflower Land. Goblin just borrowed 'em to make counting coins less boring. 🍂

I'm just a regular Goblin who plays the game like you, got tired of guessin' numbers on me own farm, and built this lil tool in me workshop to help figure it all out.

Any farmer — big, small, or still growin' their first cauliflower — can use GOBLIN CALCULATOR to sniff out real profit before spendin' hard-earned coins on a shiny new boost. Test it here first, buy it there later. Smart goblin business, that.

This calculator is a mimicker, heh heh — it just copies how your farm flows, so feed it your real numbers and make good use of it, ya hear?

My IGN's LAKAY. If this lil gadget saved ya some coin (or some headache), toss my farm a cheer: sunflower-land.com/play/?ref=LAKAY
Now go on, git farmin'! 🥕

⚡ Boosts

Tap a boost to switch it on. Active boosts change yield, grow time, and cost per unit everywhere in the app — instantly.
Enter how many plots you have per crop, how many tool-uses ("nodes") you have per resource, and how many trees ("nodes") you have per fruit. This powers the Total Plots/Trees & Harvest numbers shown below and in the Resources/Fruits panels. Crop plots and every Resource node count are required before any AoE boost (like Gnome on crops or Sir Goldensnout on resources) can be turned on, so the boost gets diluted across your real plot/node count instead of being applied as if every single plot/node got the full bonus. Fruit node counts and animals are optional — fill them in too if you want accurate fruit dilution, but leaving them at 0 won't block boosts, it'll just mean that specific fruit shows "0 effect right now" for an AoE boost until you enter its node count.
🌾 Crop plots (required)
⛏️ Resource nodes (tool uses) (required)
🍎 Fruit nodes (trees) (optional — 0 is fine)
🍇 Greenhouse plots (optional — 0 is fine)
🐄 Animal levels & heads (optional — 0 is fine)
Sickness & Barn Delight (estimate)
Sickness odds aren't published — enter what you actually observe: roughly how many heads of each animal type tend to get sick per day, and how many cures each of those heads needs. The app divides that across your total head count automatically, so you don't have to do the math. The cost of each cure (Barn Delight = 5 🍋 Lemon + 3 🍯 Honey) is calculated automatically from your Lemon/Honey source and any active boosts like Medic Apron.

🌿 BUD NFTs
BUDs are 1:1 NFT companions — each one has a Type, a Stem and an Aura. Pick the combination on your BUD and it's added below as its own always-on boost. Each Type/Stem/Aura combo can only be added once — you can't add two identical BUDs.
